mnemonics.title: PAGE REPLACEMENT ALGORITHMS
mnemonics.content: FIFO: FIRST IN FIRST OUT - OLDEST PAGE OUT. LRU: LEAST RECENTLY USED - OLDEST TIME STAMP OUT. OPTIMAL: OPTIMUM DECISION - FUTURE KNOWLEDGE.

mnemonics.title: VIRTUAL MEMORY BENEFITS
mnemonics.content: VIRTUAL MEMORY = PERMITS LARGER PROGRAMS, MULTIPLE PROGRAMS, MEMORY PROTECTION, EFFICIENT MEMORY USE.

mnemonics.title: TLB PURPOSE
mnemonics.content: TLB = TRANSLATION LOOKASIDE BUFFER = SPEEDS UP PAGING BY CACHING ADDRESS TRANSLATIONS.

acronyms.usage: Hardware cache for virtual-to-physical address translations
Page replacement algorithm that evicts oldest page
Page replacement algorithm favoring recently used pages
Page replacement algorithm favoring recently used pages
Hardware device that handles memory accesses and paging
Data structure storing process information including memory details

visualTips.visualization: THINK OF A BOOK DIVIDED INTO CHAPTERS (PAGES). EACH CHAPTER CAN BE PLACED IN ANY AVAILABLE PAGE (FRAME) IN MEMORY. THE TABLE OF CONTENTS IS LIKE THE PAGE TABLE SHOWING WHERE EACH CHAPTER IS.
IMAGINE A LIBRARIAN LOOKING FOR A BOOK (PAGE) THAT IS NOT ON THE SHELF (MEMORY). THEY MUST GO TO THE STORAGE (DISK) TO GET IT - THIS DELAY IS LIKE A PAGE FAULT.
IMAGINE SOMEONE CONSTANTLY RUNNING BACK AND FORTH TO GET TOOLS FROM A DISTANT SHED (DISK) BECAUSE THEIR TOOLBOX (MEMORY) IS TOO SMALL. THEY SPEND MORE TIME FETCHING THAN WORKING.
THINK OF A BUILDING WITH DIFFERENT FLOORS (SEGMENTS) FOR DIFFERENT PURPOSES - OFFICE, RESIDENTIAL, PARKING. EACH FLOOR HAS ITS OWN LOCATION AND SIZE.

analogies.analogy: LIKE A LIBRARY USEING OFFSITE STORAGE. YOU REQUEST A BOOK, AND IF IT IS NOT IN THE MAIN LIBRARY, THEY FETCH IT FROM WAREHOUSE. YOU DO NOT KNOW WHERE THE BOOK ACTUALLY IS STORED.
LIKE A MAP OR INDEX SHOWING WHERE EACH DESTINATION (VIRTUAL PAGE) IS LOCATED IN THE CITY (PHYSICAL MEMORY). THE INDEX TELLS YOU WHICH BLOCK (FRAME) TO FIND EACH DESTINATION.
LIKE A SHORT-TERM MEMORY OF FREQUENTLY USED PHONE NUMBERS. YOU DO NOT LOOK THEM UP IN THE DIRECTORY EVERY TIME - YOU RECALL THEM QUICKLY.
TWO PEOPLE SHARING A DOCUMENT INITIALLY SEEING THE SAME COPY. ONLY WHEN ONE STARTS MAKING CHANGES DOES THE SYSTEM CREATE A SEPARATE COPY FOR EACH PERSON.
